decks.notes: This deck focuses on aggressive plays by putting pressure on the opponent with a combination of low-cost creatures like Scion of Draco and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, supported by efficient removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Leyline Binding. The deck aims to control the battlefield and maintain a strong board presence while disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Quantum Riddler and Stubborn Denial. Additionally, the deck utilizes land cards like Flooded Strand and Wooded Foothills to ensure consistent mana production for both casting spells and activating abilities. The overall strategy revolves around fast-paced aggression and disruption.

The deck focuses on quickly reanimating powerful creatures like Griselbrand or Atraxa, Grand Unifier from the graveyard using cards like Goryo's Vengeance, while also disrupting the opponent with cards like Thoughtseize and Solitude. It also utilizes cards with flicker effects such as Ephemerate to protect its reanimated creatures or generate value by triggering enters-the-battlefield effects. The deck aims to create explosive plays and outvalue the opponent through a combination of combo pieces and disruption spells.

The deck aims to use cycling creatures and cascade spells to fill the graveyard with creatures, then cast Living End to return all creatures to the battlefield from the graveyard. The key concept is to use the combination of cycling creatures, cascade spells, and Living End to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a huge board presence. The deck focuses on manipulating the graveyard and controlling the tempo of the game to set up powerful combos and win conditions.

Utilizing disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Thought-Knot Seer, along with efficient removal spells like Fatal Push, the Mono Black Aggro deck aims to disrupt opponent's game plan while deploying threats like Emperor of Bones and Nethergoyf to quickly close out the game. The deck also employs Urza's Saga to tutor for key artifacts and land cards, giving the deck flexibility and consistency. With a focus on fast, aggressive plays and disruption, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize or mount a defense.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying artifact creatures and utilizing cards that provide additional value when artifacts are in play. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with early aggression and disruption, while also having the option for explosive plays with cards like Engineered Explosives and Arcbound Ravager. The synergy between artifacts, artifact creatures, and artifact-based spells allows for a versatile and dynamic gameplay style that can adapt to different matchups and board states. Additionally, the inclusion of Urza's Saga and Mishra's Bauble provides card advantage and consistency to ensure a streamlined and efficient game plan.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the Allosaurus Rider combo by using cards like Neoform and Eldritch Evolution to bring out creatures like Griselbrand and Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant. By generating a large amount of mana with cards like Planar Genesis and Generous Ent, the deck can cast game-ending spells like Ureni, the Song Unending to overwhelm the opponent. In addition, the deck includes interaction with Pact of Negation for protection and Consign to Memory for disruption. The deck focuses on explosive plays and utilizing the combo pieces efficiently to secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing cheap and efficient burn spells, such as Lightning Bolt, Lava Spike, and Boros Charm, to quickly deal damage to the opponent while also controlling the board with cards like Eidolon of the Great Revel. It also incorporates aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Goblin Guide to apply consistent pressure. The synergy between burn spells and aggressive creatures allows the deck to quickly reduce the opponent's life total and win the game before they have a chance to stabilize.

The deck focuses on utilizing low-cost aggressive creatures to apply pressure on the opponent while using spells to control the board and push for damage. It includes cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler to quickly deal damage, alongside spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart for removal and reach. The deck also includes cards like Expressive Iteration and Mishra's Bauble for card advantage and to help maintain tempo. With a combination of creatures and burn spells, the deck aims to win the game quickly through aggressive plays and efficient use of resources.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly assemble a combo using cards like Whir of Invention and Goblin Charbelcher without relying on lands. It aims to control the game with disruptive spells like Counterspell and Sink into Stupor while tutoring for key pieces with cards like Waterlogged Teachings. The deck also includes ways to protect the combo with cards like Force of Negation and Jwari Disruption. Overall, the deck focuses on efficiently executing its win condition while disrupting the opponent's strategy.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on fast and aggressive creature plays, using cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to overwhelm the opponent quickly. It also includes burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Discharge for added reach. The deck includes disruptive cards like Static Prison and Voice of Victory to control the board and protect its threats. With a mix of creatures, burn spells, and disruption, this deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain aggression throughout the game to secure a victory.

This deck focuses on fast, aggressive play by utilizing low-cost spells and creatures to deal damage quickly. It combines burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart with efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler. Mishra's Bauble and Preordain help to draw cards and dig for key pieces, while Boomerang Basics and Stormchaser's Talent provide additional support. The mana base includes fetch lands like Scalding Tarn and Bloodstained Mire to ensure consistent color access. Overall, the strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with a constant barrage of damage.

The Boros Aggro deck focuses on a fast and aggressive playstyle, utilizing creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride to quickly deal damage to the opponent. Key cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer provide additional support and removal options. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Static Prison and Goblin Bombardment to control the board and create openings for lethal attacks. The inclusion of cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Thraben Charm offer versatility and resilience against opposing strategies, making it a well-rounded and formidable aggro deck in the Modern format.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of counterspells, removal, and card draw. It aims to delay the opponent's game plan while efficiently answering threats, ultimately gaining card advantage and stabilizing the board. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion adds a secondary win condition and provides additional value. The deck's mana base allows for consistency in casting its spells and accessing different colors of mana when needed. Overall, the deck is designed to outvalue and outplay the opponent in a slow and methodical manner.

The main objective of this deck is to quickly cycle through cards like Street Wraith and Generous Ent to get as many creatures into the graveyard as possible. By casting Living End, you can then bring all those creatures back onto the battlefield, overwhelming your opponent with a powerful board presence. The deck focuses on utilizing combos like Violent Outburst to quickly execute this strategy and win the game. It also includes disruption in the form of Force of Negation and other control elements to protect the combo and maintain board control.

The main concept of this Boros Ponza control deck is to disrupt the opponent's lands and resources while maintaining board control and generating value through spells like Cleansing Wildfire and Erode. The deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan, while utilizing removal spells like Path to Exile and Wrath of God to deal with threats. The inclusion of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion adds an additional creature to the deck for extra value. Overall, the deck focuses on resource denial and controlling the game until it can overpower the opponent.

This deck focuses on utilizing a combination of disruptive spells, efficient removal, and controlling tactics to outmaneuver opponents. By using cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to disrupt the opponent's game plan, alongside powerful creatures like Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares, the deck aims to control the pace of the game and grind out incremental advantages. Additionally, the deck includes counterspells like Counterspell and Subtlety to protect its own threats and maintain a tight grip on the board. Overall, the deck excels at controlling the game and outlasting opponents.
